iti76:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti76:2.1: “Aspiring to these three forms of bliss, monks, a wise person should guard his virtue.
iti76:2.3:
[Thinking,] ‘May praise come to me,’ a wise person should guard his virtue.
[Thinking,] ‘May wealth come to me,’ a wise person should guard his virtue.
[Thinking,] ‘At the break-up of the body, after death, may I reappear in a good destination,
a heavenly world,’ a wise person should guard his virtue.
iti76:2.4: Aspiring to these three forms of bliss, a wise person should guard his virtue.”
iti76:3.1: Intelligent, you should guard your virtue,
iti76:3.2: aspiring to three forms of bliss:
iti76:3.4: and, after death, rejoicing in heaven.
iti76:4.1: Even if you do no evil
iti76:4.2: but seek out one who does,
iti76:5.1: The sort of person you make a friend,
iti76:5.3: that’s the sort you yourself become–
iti76:5.4: for your living together is of that sort.
iti76:6.1: The one associated with, the one who associates,
iti76:6.2: the one who’s touched, the one who touches another
iti76:6.6: should not be comrades with evil people.
iti76:7.1: A man who wraps rotting fish in a blade of kusa grass
iti76:7.4: so it is if you seek out fools.
iti76:8.1: But a man who wraps powdered incense in the leaf of a tree
iti76:8.4: so it is if you seek out the enlightened.
iti76:9.1: So, knowing your own outcome as like the leaf-wrapper’s,
iti76:9.3: you shouldn’t seek out those who aren’t good.
iti76:9.4: The wise would associate with those who are.
iti76:9.5: Those who aren’t good lead you to hell.
iti76:9.6: The good help you reach a good destination.
